title: Invalid Character Validation in a String

statement.markdown:
Given a string `s`, return `true` if it contains all unique characters and is a palindrome. A string is a palindrome if it reads the same forwards and backwards. If the string is empty, it is considered a palindrome.

**Example Cases**

*   Input: `s = "abcba
statement.inputFormat: A string `s` of length up to 1000 containing only ASCII characters.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the string is a palindrome with all unique characters.
statement.notes: If the string is empty, it is considered a palindrome.

examples.explanation: The string is a palindrome with all unique characters.
The string is not a palindrome with all unique characters.

hints.text: Use the two-pointer technique to compare characters from the start and end of the string.

editorial.title: Invalid Character Validation in a String - Editorial
editorial.intuition: We can use the two-pointer technique to compare characters from the start and end of the string. If the string is a palindrome, the characters at the start and end indices should be the same.
editorial.bruteForce: We can create a new string that contains all unique characters from the input string. Then, we can check if the new string is a palindrome by comparing characters from the start and end of the string.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use the two-pointer technique to compare characters from the start and end of the string. If the string is a palindrome, the characters at the start and end indices should be the same. We can also keep track of unique characters in a set.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n) because we only iterate through the string once. The space complexity is O(m) where m is the number of unique characters in the string.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(string s); };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean solve(String s); }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(string s) { int i = 0, j = s.size() - 1; for (i < j; i < j; i++) { if (s[i] == s[j]) continue; else return false; } return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean solve(String s) { int i = 0, j = s.length() - 1; for (i < j; i < j; i++) { if (s.charAt(i) == s.charAt(j)) continue; else return false; } return true; } }

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def solve(self, s: str) -> bool:
        i = 0
        j = len(s) - 1
        while i < j:
            if s[i] != s[j]:
                return False
            i += 1
            j -= 1
        return True

# Helper function to read input from stdin
import sys
sys.stdin = open(0)
s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
print(Solution().solve(s))
